title: Add CycloneDX SBOM and Sigstore attestation to CI
description:
**Size:** 📦 medium
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high
**Source:** Proposed for 'From the start, SBOMs with CycloneDX should be generated for all'

Integrate SBOM generation and attestation into `.github/workflows/build-images.yaml` for all container images:

1. **`build-base-images` job**: Add `id-token: write` and `attestations: write` permissions. After `build-python-base` and `build-venv-builder` push steps, add two new step pairs each:
   - `anchore/sbom-action` (SHA-pinned) scanning the pushed image by digest (`steps.build-python-base.outputs.digest` / `steps.build-venv-builder.outputs.digest`), outputting CycloneDX 1.5 JSON (`format: cyclonedx-json`)
   - `actions/attest-sbom@v2` (SHA-pinned) attesting the SBOM via Sigstore keyless OIDC and pushing attestation to GHCR (`push-to-registry: true`)
   - Both steps guarded by `if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'`

2. **`build-service-images` job**: Same permissions additions. After the `build-service` step, add one SBOM generation + attestation step pair referencing `steps.build-service.outputs.digest` and `matrix.service` for naming. Same PR-skip guard.

3. **SHA-pinning**: Both new actions must be SHA-pinned with version comments per existing convention enforced by `verify_build_images_workflow.sh`.

4. **Verification tests**: Update `tests/container-images/verify_build_images_workflow.sh` with new test cases validating: SBOM generation steps exist with `anchore/sbom-action` and correct `format: cyclonedx-json` parameter; attestation steps exist with `actions/attest-sbom` and `push-to-registry: true`; `id-token: write` and `attestations: write` permissions on both build jobs; `if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'` guard on all SBOM/attestation steps. Use existing assertion helpers from `tests/lib/assertions.sh`.

**Rationale:** Container images published to GHCR currently lack any software bill of materials or cryptographic attestation, leaving consumers unable to verify image contents or provenance. The architecture doc (`architecture/docs/08-container-images/04-sbom.md`) mandates CycloneDX SBOMs with Sigstore attestation. Without this, the project cannot meet supply chain security requirements, and downstream consumers have no tamper-evident way to audit image dependencies. This is a prerequisite for any future vulnerability scanning or compliance reporting from SBOMs.

**Affected Areas:**
- .github/workflows/build-images.yaml
- tests/container-images/verify_build_images_workflow.sh
- architecture/docs/08-container-images/04-sbom.md

stories.title: Image consumer verifies image provenance via SBOM attestation
stories.want: to verify the cryptographic attestation of a container image's SBOM before deployment
stories.so_that: I can trust that the image contents have not been tampered with since build time
criteria: Running `gh attestation verify oci://ghcr.io/<owner>/<service>:<tag> --owner <owner>` succeeds for images pushed from main/stable branches
Attestation is stored as an OCI referrer artifact alongside the image in GHCR
Attestation is signed via Sigstore keyless OIDC bound to the GitHub Actions workflow identity
No attestation exists for images built from pull requests

stories.title: Image consumer inspects software components via CycloneDX SBOM
stories.want: to obtain a CycloneDX 1.5 JSON SBOM listing all software components in a container image
stories.so_that: I can audit dependencies, check for known vulnerabilities, and meet compliance requirements
criteria: SBOM is generated in CycloneDX 1.5 JSON format using Syft (anchore/sbom-action)
SBOM covers both OS packages (dpkg) and Python packages (dist-info) in the final image
SBOM is generated for all three image types: python-base, venv-builder, and service images
SBOM can be extracted from the attestation using cosign verify-attestation

stories.title: CI workflow generates SBOMs for all pushed container images
stories.want: SBOM generation and attestation to be integrated into the existing build-images workflow
stories.so_that: every image pushed to GHCR automatically receives a signed SBOM without manual intervention
criteria: SBOM generation step added after python-base push in build-base-images job
SBOM generation step added after venv-builder push in build-base-images job
SBOM generation step added after build-service step in build-service-images job
Each SBOM generation step is followed by a corresponding attestation step
Both new actions (anchore/sbom-action, actions/attest-sbom) are SHA-pinned with version comments

stories.title: PR builds remain unaffected by SBOM generation
stories.want: pull request builds to skip SBOM generation and attestation entirely
stories.so_that: PR CI time is not increased and no unnecessary attestations are created for ephemeral builds
criteria: All SBOM generation steps have `if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'` guard
All attestation steps have `if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'` guard
PR builds continue to work identically to before this change
No OIDC token requests occur during PR builds

stories.title: Workflow maintainer validates SBOM configuration via verification tests
stories.want: verify_build_images_workflow.sh to validate all SBOM/attestation configuration
stories.so_that: misconfiguration of SBOM generation or attestation is caught before merge
criteria: Tests verify id-token: write and attestations: write permissions on both build jobs
Tests verify SBOM generation steps exist with correct action and format parameter
Tests verify attestation steps exist with correct action and push-to-registry parameter
Tests verify PR-skip guard is present on all SBOM/attestation steps
Tests use existing assertion helpers from tests/lib/assertions.sh

requirements.description: The workflow SHALL generate CycloneDX 1.5 JSON SBOMs using anchore/sbom-action for every image pushed to GHCR
requirements.rationale: CycloneDX 1.5 is mandated by architecture/docs/08-container-images/04-sbom.md for supply chain transparency and vulnerability tracking

scenarios.name: SBOM generated for python-base on push
scenarios.when: the build-base-images job runs on a push event and python-base is pushed to GHCR
scenarios.then: anchore/sbom-action scans the python-base image by digest and produces a CycloneDX JSON file
and_then: the output file is named sbom-python-base.cyclonedx.json

scenarios.name: SBOM generated for venv-builder on push
scenarios.when: the build-base-images job runs on a push event and venv-builder is pushed to GHCR
scenarios.then: anchore/sbom-action scans the venv-builder image by digest and produces a CycloneDX JSON file
and_then: the output file is named sbom-venv-builder.cyclonedx.json

scenarios.name: SBOM generated for service images on push
scenarios.when: the build-service-images job runs on a push event and a service image is pushed to GHCR
scenarios.then: anchore/sbom-action scans the service image by digest and produces a CycloneDX JSON file
and_then: the output file uses the matrix service name for disambiguation

requirements.description: The workflow SHALL attest each SBOM using actions/attest-sbom@v2 with Sigstore keyless OIDC signing
requirements.rationale: Sigstore attestation provides tamper-evident provenance bound to the GitHub Actions OIDC identity, enabling consumers to verify image integrity

scenarios.name: SBOM attested for python-base
scenarios.when: the python-base SBOM has been generated
scenarios.then: actions/attest-sbom signs the SBOM with Sigstore and stores the attestation as an OCI artifact
and_then: subject-name matches the python-base image name
subject-digest matches the build step digest output

scenarios.name: SBOM attested for venv-builder
scenarios.when: the venv-builder SBOM has been generated
scenarios.then: actions/attest-sbom signs the SBOM with Sigstore and stores the attestation as an OCI artifact
and_then: subject-name matches the venv-builder image name
subject-digest matches the build step digest output

scenarios.name: SBOM attested for service images
scenarios.when: a service image SBOM has been generated
scenarios.then: actions/attest-sbom signs the SBOM with Sigstore and stores the attestation as an OCI artifact
and_then: subject-name matches the service image name
subject-digest matches the build-service step digest output

requirements.description: The build-base-images and build-service-images jobs SHALL have id-token: write and attestations: write permissions
requirements.rationale: id-token: write is required for Sigstore OIDC signing; attestations: write is required for the GitHub Attestations API to store attestations

scenarios.name: build-base-images has required permissions
scenarios.when: the build-base-images job permissions are inspected
scenarios.then: id-token: write and attestations: write are present alongside existing permissions
and_then: existing contents: read and packages: write permissions remain unchanged

scenarios.name: build-service-images has required permissions
scenarios.when: the build-service-images job permissions are inspected
scenarios.then: id-token: write and attestations: write are present alongside existing permissions
and_then: existing contents: read and packages: write permissions remain unchanged

scenarios.name: Non-build jobs permissions unchanged
scenarios.when: verify-base-images or verify-service-images job permissions are inspected
scenarios.then: no id-token or attestations permissions are added
and_then: these jobs retain their existing minimal permissions

requirements.description: All SBOM generation and attestation steps SHALL be guarded by if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'
requirements.rationale: PR builds should not generate SBOMs or attestations to avoid unnecessary OIDC token requests and attestation artifacts for ephemeral builds

scenarios.name: SBOM steps skipped on PR
scenarios.when: the workflow runs on a pull_request event
scenarios.then: all SBOM generation steps (anchore/sbom-action) are skipped
and_then: no CycloneDX JSON files are generated

scenarios.name: Attestation steps skipped on PR
scenarios.when: the workflow runs on a pull_request event
scenarios.then: all attestation steps (actions/attest-sbom) are skipped
and_then: no OIDC tokens are requested
no attestations are pushed to GHCR

scenarios.name: SBOM steps run on push
scenarios.when: the workflow runs on a push event to main or stable/**
scenarios.then: all SBOM generation and attestation steps execute
and_then: SBOMs are generated and attested for all pushed images

requirements.description: The anchore/sbom-action and actions/attest-sbom actions SHALL be SHA-pinned with version comments
requirements.rationale: SHA pinning prevents supply-chain attacks via tag mutation, matching the existing convention enforced by verify_build_images_workflow.sh

scenarios.name: anchore/sbom-action SHA-pinned
scenarios.when: the workflow YAML is inspected for anchore/sbom-action usage
scenarios.then: the action reference uses a 40-character hex SHA with a version comment (e.g., # v0)
and_then: the existing test_actions_pinned_to_sha test passes

scenarios.name: actions/attest-sbom SHA-pinned
scenarios.when: the workflow YAML is inspected for actions/attest-sbom usage
scenarios.then: the action reference uses a 40-character hex SHA with a version comment (e.g., # v2)
and_then: the existing test_actions_have_version_comments test passes

requirements.description: The workflow SHALL generate SBOMs for all three image types: python-base, venv-builder, and service images
requirements.rationale: All container images published to GHCR must have SBOMs for complete supply chain coverage as mandated by the architecture doc

scenarios.name: python-base SBOM references correct digest
scenarios.when: the SBOM generation step for python-base is configured
scenarios.then: the image parameter references steps.build-python-base.outputs.digest
and_then: the scanned image matches exactly the image pushed to GHCR

scenarios.name: venv-builder SBOM references correct digest
scenarios.when: the SBOM generation step for venv-builder is configured
scenarios.then: the image parameter references steps.build-venv-builder.outputs.digest
and_then: the scanned image matches exactly the image pushed to GHCR

scenarios.name: Service image SBOM references correct digest
scenarios.when: the SBOM generation step for a service image is configured
scenarios.then: the image parameter references steps.build-service.outputs.digest
and_then: the scanned image matches exactly the image pushed to GHCR

requirements.description: The attestation SHALL be pushed to GHCR as an OCI referrer artifact via push-to-registry: true
requirements.rationale: Storing attestations in GHCR alongside the image enables consumers to discover and verify attestations using standard OCI tooling (gh, cosign)

scenarios.name: Attestation pushed to registry for base images
scenarios.when: actions/attest-sbom runs for python-base or venv-builder
scenarios.then: push-to-registry is set to true
and_then: the attestation is stored as an OCI referrer artifact in GHCR

scenarios.name: Attestation pushed to registry for service images
scenarios.when: actions/attest-sbom runs for a service image
scenarios.then: push-to-registry is set to true
and_then: the attestation is discoverable via gh attestation verify

requirements.description: The verify_build_images_workflow.sh SHALL include test cases validating all SBOM/attestation configuration
requirements.rationale: Automated verification catches SBOM/attestation misconfiguration before merge, preventing silent loss of supply chain security guarantees

scenarios.name: Tests verify SBOM permissions
scenarios.when: verify_build_images_workflow.sh runs
scenarios.then: it validates id-token: write and attestations: write on both build-base-images and build-service-images
and_then: test uses yq_raw to query job permissions from the workflow YAML

scenarios.name: Tests verify SBOM generation steps
scenarios.when: verify_build_images_workflow.sh runs
scenarios.then: it validates anchore/sbom-action steps exist with format: cyclonedx-json parameter
and_then: test uses assert_contains or assert_file_contains from assertions.sh

scenarios.name: Tests verify attestation steps
scenarios.when: verify_build_images_workflow.sh runs
scenarios.then: it validates actions/attest-sbom steps exist with push-to-registry: true parameter
and_then: test uses existing assertion helpers

scenarios.name: Tests verify PR-skip guard
scenarios.when: verify_build_images_workflow.sh runs
scenarios.then: it validates all SBOM/attestation steps have if: github.event_name != 'pull_request' guard
and_then: test covers both build-base-images and build-service-images jobs

Add verification tests for attestation steps (actions/attest-sbom existence, push-to-registry: true) and PR-skip guard on all SBOM/attestation steps in verify_build_images_workflow.sh (REQ-011, REQ-013, REQ-016, REQ-017)
Update docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md with SBOM generation, attestation steps, required permissions, and PR behavior documentation (REQ-010, REQ-011, REQ-012)

review_criteria: All SBOM generation and attestation steps are present in build-base-images (2 pairs for python-base and venv-builder) and build-service-images (1 pair) jobs
Both new actions (anchore/sbom-action, actions/attest-sbom) are SHA-pinned to 40-char hex SHA with version comments matching existing # vN convention
id-token: write and attestations: write permissions are added to build-base-images and build-service-images but NOT to verify-base-images or verify-service-images
All 6 SBOM/attestation steps (3 SBOM + 3 attest) have `if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'` guard
Existing tests (test_actions_pinned_to_sha, test_actions_have_version_comments, test_run_blocks_use_env_vars) pass without modification
All new test functions in verify_build_images_workflow.sh are invoked in the run-all-tests section and pass against the modified workflow
SBOM generation steps reference the correct digest output for each image (build-python-base, build-venv-builder, build-service)
docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md documents the new SBOM/attestation steps, required permissions, and PR-skip behavior
implementation_notes:
Architectural decisions:

1. **Step placement**: SBOM generation steps go immediately after each build-push-action step, followed by the corresponding attestation step. In build-base-images: python-base SBOM+attest after build-python-base, venv-builder SBOM+attest after build-venv-builder. In build-service-images: service SBOM+attest after build-service (before the inline PR verification step).

2. **Image owner for service images**: build-service-images lacks a normalized owner output (unlike build-base-images which has steps.meta.outputs.owner). Extend the existing 'Derive tags' step (id: tags) to also output `image` — the `ghcr.io/${IMAGE_OWNER}/${MATRIX_SERVICE}` path already computed as the `IMAGE` variable. Add `echo "image=${IMAGE}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"` and reference as `steps.tags.outputs.image` in SBOM/attestation `with:` parameters.

3. **Output file naming**: Use distinct filenames to avoid collisions within a job: `sbom-python-base.cyclonedx.json` and `sbom-venv-builder.cyclonedx.json` for base images; `sbom-${{ matrix.service }}.cyclonedx.json` for service images. Using `matrix.service` in `with:` context is safe (not a shell injection vector).

4. **SHA pins**: Look up the latest release commit SHAs at implementation time:
   - anchore/sbom-action: check https://github.com/anchore/sbom-action/tags for latest v0.x release tag, get full commit SHA
   - actions/attest-sbom: check https://github.com/actions/attest-sbom/tags for latest v2.x release tag, get full commit SHA
   Pin format: `uses: <org>/<action>@<40-char-sha> # v<version>` (must match test_actions_pinned_to_sha and test_actions_have_version_comments expectations)

5. **Expression injection safety**: The new SBOM/attestation steps use `with:` parameters (YAML values in GitHub Actions expression context), not `run:` blocks, so shell injection is not a concern. The only `run:` block change is adding an `echo` to the existing tags step, which already uses env vars per the expression injection defense pattern.

6. **Test structure**: Follow the existing pattern in verify_build_images_workflow.sh — one bash function per test concern, using yq_raw() for YAML queries and assert_* helpers for assertions. Add CC-0029 reference in test function echo headers. Add test function calls to the 'Run all tests' section at the bottom of the file.

7. **Permissions scope**: id-token: write and attestations: write are added only to the two build jobs (build-base-images, build-service-images). Verification jobs (verify-base-images, verify-service-images) retain their minimal permissions. The test should verify this boundary.

Potential pitfalls:
- Forgetting to add new test function calls to the run-all-tests section at the bottom of verify_build_images_workflow.sh
- Using an anchore/sbom-action parameter name that differs from the pinned version's action.yml
- Not adding the image output to the tags step in build-service-images, leaving SBOM/attestation steps without a lowercase image name for GHCR
